title: Keyboard or Mouse Not Working
cardText: Diagnose keyboards and mice that do not respond, disconnect unexpectedly, or are not detected.
Check power, batteries, connections, drivers, Bluetooth, wireless receivers, and hardware faults.
Separate device faults from operating system and driver issues.
text: This guide is for keyboards and mice that are not responding or are behaving unexpectedly.
Check simple issues such as batteries, connections, and power before investigating drivers.
Determine whether the issue affects one device or multiple input devices.
Escalate hardware faults after software troubleshooting is complete.

identifyAffectedDevice.title: Identify the affected device
body: Determine whether the issue affects a keyboard, mouse, or both.
If both devices fail at the same time, the cause is often the computer rather than the devices themselves.

checkConnectionType.title: Identify connection type
body: Determine whether the device is USB, wireless, or Bluetooth.
Different connection methods require different troubleshooting.

checkUsbConnection.title: Check USB connection
body: Reconnect the device.
Try a different USB port.
Avoid USB hubs during testing.

checkWirelessPower.title: Check batteries and power
body: Confirm the device is switched on.
Replace or recharge batteries.
Check for power indicator lights.

replaceBatteries.title: Replace batteries or recharge device
body: Low battery power is a common cause of keyboard and mouse failures.
Replace or recharge the batteries and test again.

checkWirelessReceiver.title: Check wireless receiver
body: If the device uses a USB receiver, ensure it is connected.
Try another USB port if available.
Look for physical damage.

receiverFault.title: Wireless receiver issue found
body: The wireless receiver appears faulty or disconnected.
Reconnect or replace the receiver if supported.

checkBluetoothConnection.title: Check Bluetooth connection
body: Verify the device is paired.
Remove and re-pair the device if necessary.
Bluetooth devices may disconnect after updates or power events.

repairBluetooth.title: Repair Bluetooth connection
body: Remove the device and pair it again.
Retest input after pairing completes.

testAlternateInput.title: Test another keyboard or mouse
body: Connect a known-good keyboard or mouse.
This helps determine whether the issue is with the device or the computer.

deviceFault.title: Input device fault found
body: The replacement device works correctly.
The original keyboard or mouse is likely faulty.

checkUsbPorts.title: Check USB ports
body: Test the ports using another USB device.
Failed ports can affect multiple keyboards and mice.

usbPortFault.title: USB port issue found
body: The USB ports are not functioning correctly.
Investigate USB controller drivers, motherboard ports, or physical damage.

checkDeviceManager.title: Check Device Manager
body: Open Device Manager.
Look for warning symbols, disabled devices, or missing keyboard/mouse entries.
Driver issues commonly appear here.

reinstallDrivers.title: Reinstall device drivers
body: Remove the affected device from Device Manager.
Restart the computer and allow Windows to reinstall it.
Retest the device afterwards.

checkSafeMode.title: Test in Safe Mode
body: Boot into Safe Mode if possible.
If the device works there, software or driver conflicts may be responsible.

softwareConflict.title: Software conflict likely
body: The device works in Safe Mode.
Investigate startup software, drivers, utilities, or recent updates.

hardwareEscalation.title: Further hardware diagnostics required
body: Power, drivers, ports, Bluetooth, and replacement device testing did not resolve the issue.
Possible causes include motherboard faults, USB controller failures, or internal hardware problems.
Escalate for hardware diagnostics.
